message:
bd-7xja: reject zero stripe_len in btrfs chunk parsing

A btrfs chunk item with stripe_len == 0 is structurally invalid: every
RAID profile divides a logical address by stripe_len to locate the
owning stripe, so accepting a zero value would either panic on the
divide or silently route all offsets to stripe 0.

parse_chunk_item now rejects such chunks with a dedicated
ParseError::InvalidField { field: "stripe_len", reason: "chunk has zero
stripe length" }, alongside the pre-existing zero-stripe-count check.

Added parse_chunk_item_rejects_zero_stripe_len unit test covering a
hand-built single-stripe chunk whose header is valid in every other
respect — length, owner, chunk_type, io_align, io_width, sector_size,
num_stripes=1, sub_stripes=0, and a well-formed stripe entry — so the
test fails only when the stripe_len guard is removed.

Tracked in .beads as bd-7xja (in_progress).

Leaving scratch reports in the working tree untracked:
clippy-pedantic.txt, clippy_report.txt, fresh-ubs.txt, pedantic_clippy.txt,
indexing_slicing.txt, unwraps2.txt, unwraps3.txt, get_hash.rs.

 {"id":"bd-7tn4","title":"PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)","description":"# PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)

## GOAL
Implement Proofs of Retrievability (PoR) for cryptographic verification that stored data is intact and retrievable, without reading the entire dataset.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 11.11: PoR (Juels-Kaliski 2007, Shacham-Waters 2008) allows a verifier to challenge a storage system with random queries. The storage system responds with proofs that specific blocks exist and are correct. If the proofs verify, the entire dataset is retrievable with high probability.

## DESIGN
- Challenge: verifier sends random block indices + random coefficients
- Response: storage computes linear combination of challenged blocks weighted by coefficients
- Verification: verifier checks response against precomputed authenticators
- Authenticators: computed once at file creation, stored alongside data

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: useful for cloud/remote storage verification
- Complementary to RaptorQ: PoR proves data exists, RaptorQ repairs it if it doesn't
- Useful for auditing: periodic PoR challenges prove filesystem integrity without full scan

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PoR authenticator generation at file creation
- [ ] Challenge-response protocol implemented
- [ ] Verification: false positive rate < 2^-128
- [ ] Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file","acceptance_criteria":"PoR authenticator generation at file creation time. Challenge-response protocol implemented with configurable challenge count. Verification: false positive rate below 2^-128. Authenticators stored alongside data in reserved metadata area. Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file. Benchmark: authenticator generation time per MB. Unit tests: generate authenticators, challenge, verify for small files. Unit tests: corrupted block causes verification failure. Unit tests: uncorrupted file passes all challenges. Property test: random challenge sets always verify on intact data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:32.840799452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585683263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585575381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","durability","por"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.828805088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-820","title":"Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush","description":"# Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush

## GOAL
Ensure repair symbols are regenerated when block groups are modified, as mandated by spec §0.4: "A block write that never triggers repair symbol refresh for its group is a spec-conformance bug."

## BACKGROUND
Currently repair symbols are generated at image creation. When blocks are modified:
1. Group becomes "stale" (symbols no longer match data)
2. Must regenerate before corruption can be recovered
3. Two modes: lazy (on next scrub) or eager (on fsync)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Dirty Group Tracking
```rust
pub struct DirtyGroupTracker {
    dirty: BitVec,  // One bit per group
}

impl DirtyGroupTracker {
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn is_dirty(&self, group: GroupNumber) -> bool;
    pub fn clear(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn dirty_groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = GroupNumber>;
}
```

### 2. Refresh Triggers
- **Lazy**: Background scrub regenerates symbols for dirty groups
- **Eager (fsync)**: Regenerate immediately for committed groups
- **Configurable**: MountConfig.repair_refresh_policy

### 3. Refresh Flow
```rust
pub fn refresh_group_symbols(
    group: GroupNumber,
    device: &dyn BlockDevice,
    storage: &mut RepairGroupStorage,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<()> {
    // 1. Read all blocks in group
    // 2. Regenerate RaptorQ symbols
    // 3. Write to repair storage slots
    // 4. Clear dirty flag
}
```

### 4. Budget Integration
- Refresh respects Cx budget
- Can be interrupted and resumed
- Progress tracked per-group

## TESTS
1. Unit: Dirty tracking set/clear
2. Integration: Write marks group dirty
3. Integration: Scrub refreshes dirty group
4. Integration: fsync refreshes if eager mode
5. E2E: Corruption after refresh -> recovery works

## LOGGING
- Mark dirty (trace): group number, cause
- Refresh start (debug): group, block count
- Refresh complete (info): group, duration, symbol count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Every modified group eventually refreshed
2. Lazy mode: refreshed within scrub interval
3. Eager mode: refreshed on fsync
4. Recovery works for refreshed groups","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:43.407560046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778165832Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778143631Z","close_reason":"Implemented dirty-group refresh APIs (mark/query/flush), aligned refresh logging, and added flush-path + tracker tests in ffs-repair pipeline; validated via rch cargo test/check/clippy and cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cow","raptorq","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.818428330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:49.899554131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-820","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:30Z"}]}
